summary: Iteration 19 - Auth Routes Refactoring Testing Complete. All auth endpoints working correctly after migration from server.py to routes/auth.py. Backend and frontend fully functional.
passed_tests: GET /api/auth/me - Returns {user: null, is_authenticated: false} for unauthenticated users
GET /api/auth/me - Handles invalid bearer tokens correctly
GET /api/categories - Returns exactly 9 categories (movie_stars, tv_actors, tv_personalities, musicians, athletes, royals, reality_tv, public_figure, other)
GET /api/hot-celebs - Returns list of hot celebrities with prices and news_premium indicators
GET /api/transfer-window-status - Returns proper status with is_open boolean and next_window
POST /api/auth/logout - Works correctly even without session
POST /api/auth/magic-link/send - Validates email format (422 for invalid)
POST /api/auth/magic-link/send - Accepts valid email and returns success
Frontend loads correctly with Hot Celebs banner visible
Search bar is visible and functional
Sign In button is visible and clickable
Auth modal appears with Google OAuth and magic link options
critical_code_review_comments: Auth routes successfully migrated to /app/backend/routes/auth.py (449 lines)
Shared database config properly extracted to /app/backend/config/database.py
Router correctly imported and registered in server.py via 'from routes import auth_router'
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_19_auth_refactor.py - Created new test file for auth refactoring
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth routes have been successfully refactored from the monolithic server.py (5080 lines) to modular routes/auth.py (449 lines). The auth router handles: GET /api/auth/me, POST /api/auth/magic-link/send, POST /api/auth/magic-link/verify, POST /api/auth/google/callback, POST /api/auth/guest/convert, POST /api/auth/logout, POST /api/auth/session. All endpoints tested and working. Frontend auth modal displays correctly with Google OAuth and magic link options.

refactoring_verification.router_integration: Working - imported via 'from routes import auth_router' in server.py
